paper_title: Novel pollen analogue technique shows bumblebees display low floral constancy and prefer sites with high floral diversity

methods.description: This method addresses the question of which and how many flowering plant species are visited by free-flying bumblebees under natural conditions. It involves collecting bumblebees, extracting pollen loads, and identifying the species present in the pollen loads.

steps.description: The entrance holes of both bumblebee nests, QN and SN, were modified to create a trap mechanism that allowed a single forager bee returning to the nest to be collected.
steps.searchable_summary: Modify bumblebee nest entrances to trap returning forager bees for collection

steps.description: Bees were collected between 9:00 and 16:00 h on specified days with suitable weather conditions.
steps.searchable_summary: Collect bees during specified times and weather conditions

steps.description: Collected bees were cooled at 4°C for 2-3 min and then placed in a marking cage.
steps.searchable_summary: Cool collected bees and place in marking cage

steps.description: Pollen from both corbiculae was removed using a pin and stored at 4°C.
steps.searchable_summary: Remove and store pollen from bee corbiculae

steps.description: Intertegular distance (ITD) of each bee was measured with a digital calliper.
The species in each pollen load were analysed by taking a slice down the centre of one pollen load from each bee sampled.
steps.searchable_summary: Analyze species in pollen load by slicing and sampling

steps.description: The subsample was homogenized in 70% EtOH, allowed to dry, and fixed in glycerol jelly.
steps.searchable_summary: Homogenize pollen subsample and fix in glycerol jelly

steps.description: Slides were inspected at 400X magnification on a Zeiss compound light microscope, and all pollen grains present were identified to the lowest taxonomic level possible.
steps.searchable_summary: Inspect and identify pollen grains under microscope

methods.description: This method uses UV fluorescent pigment as a pollen analogue to identify bumblebee foraging locations by marking all open flowers in a meadow with a single color of pigment powder.

steps.description: All open flowers in a meadow were marked with a single color of UV fluorescent pigment powder.
steps.searchable_summary: Mark all open flowers with UV fluorescent pigment

steps.description: Pollen load samples collected after UVP application were analyzed for the presence of UVPs.
steps.searchable_summary: Analyze pollen load samples for UV pigment presence
